pathogens.name: Measles
pathogens.description: Highly contagious viral infection causing fever, cough, and a red rash. Mostly controlled via vaccination but prone to local outbreaks.

pathogens.name: Dengue
pathogens.description: Tropical disease transmitted by Aedes mosquitoes, causing high fever, severe headache, and joint pain. Prevalent in seasonal warm climates.

pathogens.name: Norovirus
pathogens.description: Highly contagious gastrointestinal virus causing acute gastroenteritis. Often spreads in crowded settings and mass gatherings.

pathogens.name: Influenza
pathogens.description: Seasonal respiratory infection causing fever, body aches, and fatigue. Monitored for potential off-season spikes during major travel events.

pathogens.name: Mpox
pathogens.description: Viral disease causing painful rash, fever, and swollen lymph nodes, spread by close physical contact or contaminated items.

pathogens.name: Ebola
pathogens.description: Severe, often fatal illness spread by contact with bodily fluids. Monitored for global traveler surveillance; rare but high-severity alert pathogen.

pathogens.name: COVID-19
pathogens.description: Widespread respiratory virus causing variable symptoms. Tracked for variant shifts and transmission increases during crowd density peaks.

pathogens.name: Total Mortality
pathogens.description: Total daily all-cause deaths, tracked as a key aggregate health indicator to detect unexpected baseline shifts.

alerts.title: Mexico Travel Advisory - Level 2: Exercise Increased Caution
Gastroenteritis Advisory Near Gillette Stadium
alerts.summary: Local health authorities report an increase in norovirus-like symptoms among travelers in the Foxborough/Boston area. Increased sanitation protocols are recommended for hospitality staff.

alerts.title: Dengue Transmission Warning - Mexico City Metro
alerts.summary: Heavy seasonal rains have led to increased standing water and higher Aedes aegypti mosquito densities. Visitors are advised to apply insect repellent and wear protective clothing.

alerts.title: Imported Measles Case Under Investigation
alerts.summary: Toronto Public Health has confirmed an imported case of measles. Potential exposure sites include public transit routes from Pearson International Airport. Public contact tracing is active.

alerts.title: Local Dengue Transmission Alert
alerts.summary: Florida Health Dept has detected local transmission of Dengue virus in Miami-Dade County. Mosquito control operations have been escalated near fan festival zones.

alerts.title: COVID-19 Variant Tracking Update
alerts.summary: Wastewater surveillance in the NY/NJ metropolitan area indicates a slight uptick in COVID-19 viral loads. Health officials urge fans to stay up-to-date with booster vaccinations.

alerts.title: Food Safety & Norovirus Reminder
alerts.summary: Vancouver Coastal Health issues hygiene directives to local food vendors and pop-ups serving World Cup tourists following a minor cluster of norovirus cases at a food market.

alerts.title: Mpox Awareness & Safety Bulletin
alerts.summary: LAC DPH has issued an educational bulletin regarding Mpox symptoms and vaccine availability ahead of the tournament's opening matches. General risk remains low.
alerts.source: LA County Department of Public Health
